content: Episodic memory banks in neural planning agents can be repurposed as a curated replay buffer for online meta-learning that actively mitigates catastrophic forgetting by only replaying contextually relevant episodic traces that match the current learning task, selected via curiosity-driven acquisition signals. This creates a self-organizing consolidation pipeline where the agent’s own curiosity automatically prioritizes which past experiences to retain for future generalization, eliminating the need for static rehearsal buffers.
source_topics: neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
Online meta-learning memory consolidation with catastrophic forgetting mitigation
trigger_topic: neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
